popup.title: Golf in the Austin Region
popup.subtitle: 20 facilities across the metro
popup.body: The Austin region has evolved into one of Texas's most interesting golf markets — combining a century of municipal history, a strong private-club culture, and a growing portfolio of resort and Hill Country layouts. Golf is playable essentially year-round.

popup.title: Hancock Golf Course
popup.subtitle: Oldest continuously operated course in Texas
popup.body: Golf has been played at this central Austin site since 1899, when it was the original Austin Country Club founded by former mayor Lewis Hancock. The nine-hole par-35 layout with its pronounced hills over Waller Creek remains one of the most walkable and historic spots in Texas golf.

popup.title: Lions Municipal Golf Course
popup.subtitle: Austin's first public course — and one of the first desegregated munis in the South
popup.body: Opened in 1924 on land leased from UT, Lions Municipal expanded to 18 holes under city management and became historically significant as one of the first de facto desegregated public courses in the South — predating Brown v. Board of Education. It still hosts the Firecracker Open, Texas's oldest amateur tournament.

popup.title: Jimmy Clay & Roy Kizer
popup.subtitle: Water sustainability in Austin golf
popup.body: Austin's municipal courses now irrigate with reclaimed wastewater at discounted rates — a key strategy under recurring drought. Roy Kizer, built on former wastewater-treatment land, routes across 80+ acres of lakes and marsh, while Jimmy Clay's Joe Finger design winds through Williamson Creek.

popup.title: Grey Rock Golf Club
popup.subtitle: Premium public golf at the edge of the Hill Country
popup.body: A Jay Morrish design formerly known as Circle C Golf Club, Grey Rock sits in southwest Austin where oak-lined fairways meet the limestone fringe of the Hill Country. As a city-owned, managed daily-fee course it bridges GolfATX's value munis and full-priced private clubs.

popup.title: Austin's golf landscape
popup.subtitle: From $20 walk-ups to resort Hill Country layouts
popup.body: With 8 municipal courses, 9 public/daily-fee options, and major resort destinations, Austin offers one of the most varied golf markets in Texas. The full research report covers climate and turf, water policy, private clubs, and spatial trends.
